| MCP:vFs1pM | # vi: ft=dosini
| MCP:vFs1pM | [main]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Enables context sensitive auto-completion. If this is disabled, all
| MCP:vFs1pM | # possible completions will be listed.
| MCP:vFs1pM | smart_completion = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Enable auto suggestions (like Fish shell).
| MCP:vFs1pM | auto_suggest = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Display the completions in several columns. (More completions will be
| MCP:vFs1pM | # visible.)
| MCP:vFs1pM | wider_completion_menu = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Do not create new connections for refreshing completions; Equivalent to
| MCP:vFs1pM | # always running with the --single-connection flag.
| MCP:vFs1pM | always_use_single_connection = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Multi-line mode allows breaking up the sql statements into multiple lines. If
| MCP:vFs1pM | # this is set to True, then the end of the statements must have a semi-colon.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # If this is set to False then sql statements can't be split into multiple
| MCP:vFs1pM | # lines. End of line (return) is considered as the end of the statement.
| MCP:vFs1pM | multi_line = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # If multi_line_mode is set to "psql", in multi-line mode, [Enter] will execute
| MCP:vFs1pM | # the current input if the input ends in a semicolon.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # If multi_line_mode is set to "safe", in multi-line mode, [Enter] will always
| MCP:vFs1pM | # insert a newline, and [Esc] [Enter] or [Alt]-[Enter] must be used to execute
| MCP:vFs1pM | # a command.
| MCP:vFs1pM | multi_line_mode = psql
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Destructive warning will alert you before executing a sql statement
| MCP:vFs1pM | # that may cause harm to the database such as "drop table", "drop database",
| MCP:vFs1pM | # "shutdown", "delete", or "update".
| MCP:vFs1pM | # You can pass a list of destructive commands or leave it empty if you want to skip all warnings.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # "unconditional_update" will warn you of update statements that don't have a where clause
| MCP:vFs1pM | destructive_warning = drop, shutdown, delete, truncate, alter, update, unconditional_update
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # When `destructive_warning` is on and the user declines to proceed with a
| MCP:vFs1pM | # destructive statement, the current transaction (if any) is left untouched,
| MCP:vFs1pM | # by default. When setting `destructive_warning_restarts_connection` to
| MCP:vFs1pM | # "True", the connection to the server is restarted. In that case, the
| MCP:vFs1pM | # transaction (if any) is rolled back.
| MCP:vFs1pM | destructive_warning_restarts_connection = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # When this option is on (and if `destructive_warning` is not empty),
| MCP:vFs1pM | # destructive statements are not executed when outside of a transaction.
| MCP:vFs1pM | destructive_statements_require_transaction = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Enables expand mode, which is similar to `\x` in psql.
| MCP:vFs1pM | expand = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Enables auto expand mode, which is similar to `\x auto` in psql.
| MCP:vFs1pM | auto_expand = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Auto-retry queries on connection failures and other operational errors. If
| MCP:vFs1pM | # False, will prompt to rerun the failed query instead of auto-retrying.
| MCP:vFs1pM | auto_retry_closed_connection = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # If set to True, table suggestions will include a table alias
| MCP:vFs1pM | generate_aliases = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Path to a json file that specifies specific table aliases to use when generate_aliases is set to True
| MCP:vFs1pM | # the format for this file should be:
| MCP:vFs1pM | # {
| MCP:vFs1pM | #     "some_table_name": "desired_alias",
| MCP:vFs1pM | #     "some_other_table_name": "another_alias"
| MCP:vFs1pM | # }
| MCP:vFs1pM | alias_map_file =
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # log_file location.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # In Unix/Linux: ~/.config/pgcli/log
| MCP:vFs1pM | # In Windows: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\dbcli\pgcli\log
| MCP:vFs1pM | # %USERPROFILE% is typically C:\Users\{username}
| MCP:vFs1pM | log_file = default
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # keyword casing preference. Possible values: "lower", "upper", "auto"
| MCP:vFs1pM | keyword_casing = auto
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # casing_file location.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # In Unix/Linux: ~/.config/pgcli/casing
| MCP:vFs1pM | # In Windows: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\dbcli\pgcli\casing
| MCP:vFs1pM | # %USERPROFILE% is typically C:\Users\{username}
| MCP:vFs1pM | casing_file = default
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # If generate_casing_file is set to True and there is no file in the above
| MCP:vFs1pM | # location, one will be generated based on usage in SQL/PLPGSQL functions.
| MCP:vFs1pM | generate_casing_file = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Casing of column headers based on the casing_file described above
| MCP:vFs1pM | case_column_headers = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # history_file location.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # In Unix/Linux: ~/.config/pgcli/history
| MCP:vFs1pM | # In Windows: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\dbcli\pgcli\history
| MCP:vFs1pM | # %USERPROFILE% is typically C:\Users\{username}
| MCP:vFs1pM | history_file = default
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Default log level. Possible values: "CRITICAL", "ERROR", "WARNING", "INFO"
| MCP:vFs1pM | # and "DEBUG". "NONE" disables logging.
| MCP:vFs1pM | log_level = INFO
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Order of columns when expanding * to column list
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Possible values: "table_order" and "alphabetic"
| MCP:vFs1pM | asterisk_column_order = table_order
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Whether to qualify with table alias/name when suggesting columns
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Possible values: "always", "never" and "if_more_than_one_table"
| MCP:vFs1pM | qualify_columns = if_more_than_one_table
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # When no schema is entered, only suggest objects in search_path
| MCP:vFs1pM | search_path_filter = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Default pager. See https://www.pgcli.com/pager for more information on settings.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # By default 'PAGER' environment variable is used. If the pager is less, and the 'LESS'
| MCP:vFs1pM | # environment variable is not set, then LESS='-SRXF' will be automatically set.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # pager = less
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Timing of sql statements and table rendering.
| MCP:vFs1pM | timing = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Hide the query text when executing named queries (\n <name>).
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Only the query results will be displayed.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Can be toggled at runtime with \nq command.
| MCP:vFs1pM | hide_named_query_text = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Show/hide the informational toolbar with function keymap at the footer.
| MCP:vFs1pM | show_bottom_toolbar = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Table format. Possible values: psql, plain, simple, grid, fancy_grid, pipe,
| MCP:vFs1pM | # ascii, double, github, orgtbl, rst, mediawiki, html, latex, latex_booktabs,
| MCP:vFs1pM | # textile, moinmoin, jira, vertical, tsv, csv, sql-insert, sql-update,
| MCP:vFs1pM | # sql-update-1, sql-update-2 (formatter with sql-* prefix can format query
| MCP:vFs1pM | # output to executable insertion or updating sql).
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Recommended: psql, fancy_grid and grid.
| MCP:vFs1pM | table_format = psql
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Syntax Style. Possible values: manni, igor, xcode, vim, autumn, vs, rrt,
| MCP:vFs1pM | # native, perldoc, borland, tango, emacs, friendly, monokai, paraiso-dark,
| MCP:vFs1pM | # colorful, murphy, bw, pastie, paraiso-light, trac, default, fruity
| MCP:vFs1pM | syntax_style = default
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Keybindings:
| MCP:vFs1pM | # When Vi mode is enabled you can use modal editing features offered by Vi in the REPL.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # When Vi mode is disabled emacs keybindings such as Ctrl-A for home and Ctrl-E
| MCP:vFs1pM | # for end are available in the REPL.
| MCP:vFs1pM | vi = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Error handling
| MCP:vFs1pM | # When one of multiple SQL statements causes an error, choose to either
| MCP:vFs1pM | # continue executing the remaining statements, or stopping
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Possible values "STOP" or "RESUME"
| MCP:vFs1pM | on_error = STOP
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Set threshold for row limit. Use 0 to disable limiting.
| MCP:vFs1pM | row_limit = 1000
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Truncate long text fields to this value for tabular display (does not apply to csv).
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Leave unset to disable truncation. Example: "max_field_width = "
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Be aware that formatting might get slow with values larger than 500 and tables with
| MCP:vFs1pM | # lots of records.
| MCP:vFs1pM | max_field_width = 500
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Skip intro on startup and goodbye on exit
| MCP:vFs1pM | less_chatty = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Show all Postgres error fields (as listed in
| MCP:vFs1pM | # https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/protocol-error-fields.html).
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Can be toggled with \v.
| MCP:vFs1pM | verbose_errors = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Postgres prompt
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \t - Current date and time
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \u - Username
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \h - Short hostname of the server (up to first '.')
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \H - Hostname of the server
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \d - Database name
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \p - Database port
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \i - Postgres PID
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \# - "@" sign if logged in as superuser, '>' in other case
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \n - Newline
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \T - Transaction status: '*' if in a valid transaction, '!' if in a failed transaction, '?' if disconnected, empty otherwise
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \dsn_alias - name of dsn connection string alias if -D option is used (empty otherwise)
| MCP:vFs1pM | # \x1b[...m - insert ANSI escape sequence
| MCP:vFs1pM | # eg: prompt = '\x1b[35m\u@\x1b[32m\h:\x1b[36m\d>'
| MCP:vFs1pM | prompt = '\u@\h:\d> '
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Number of lines to reserve for the suggestion menu
| MCP:vFs1pM | min_num_menu_lines = 4
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Character used to left pad multi-line queries to match the prompt size.
| MCP:vFs1pM | multiline_continuation_char = ''
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # The string used in place of a null value.
| MCP:vFs1pM | null_string = '<null>'
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # manage pager on startup
| MCP:vFs1pM | enable_pager = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Use keyring to automatically save and load password in a secure manner
| MCP:vFs1pM | keyring = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Automatically set the session time zone to the local time zone
| MCP:vFs1pM | # If unset, uses the server's time zone, which is the Postgres default
| MCP:vFs1pM | use_local_timezone = True
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Custom colors for the completion menu, toolbar, etc.
| MCP:vFs1pM | [colors]
| MCP:vFs1pM | completion-menu.completion.current = 'bg:#ffffff #000000'
| MCP:vFs1pM | completion-menu.completion = 'bg:#008888 #ffffff'
| MCP:vFs1pM | completion-menu.meta.completion.current = 'bg:#44aaaa #000000'
| MCP:vFs1pM | completion-menu.meta.completion = 'bg:#448888 #ffffff'
| MCP:vFs1pM | completion-menu.multi-column-meta = 'bg:#aaffff #000000'
| MCP:vFs1pM | scrollbar.arrow = 'bg:#003333'
| MCP:vFs1pM | scrollbar = 'bg:#00aaaa'
| MCP:vFs1pM | selected = '#ffffff bg:#6666aa'
| MCP:vFs1pM | search = '#ffffff bg:#4444aa'
| MCP:vFs1pM | search.current = '#ffffff bg:#44aa44'
| MCP:vFs1pM | bottom-toolbar = 'bg:#222222 #aaaaaa'
| MCP:vFs1pM | bottom-toolbar.off = 'bg:#222222 #888888'
| MCP:vFs1pM | bottom-toolbar.on = 'bg:#222222 #ffffff'
| MCP:vFs1pM | search-toolbar = 'noinherit bold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | search-toolbar.text = 'nobold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | system-toolbar = 'noinherit bold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | arg-toolbar = 'noinherit bold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | arg-toolbar.text = 'nobold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | bottom-toolbar.transaction.valid = 'bg:#222222 #00ff5f bold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | bottom-toolbar.transaction.failed = 'bg:#222222 #ff005f bold'
| MCP:vFs1pM | # These three values can be used to further refine the syntax highlighting.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # They are commented out by default, since they have priority over the theme set
| MCP:vFs1pM | # with the `syntax_style` setting and overriding its behavior can be confusing.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # literal.string = '#ba2121'
| MCP:vFs1pM | # literal.number = '#666666'
| MCP:vFs1pM | # keyword = 'bold #008000'
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # style classes for colored table output
| MCP:vFs1pM | output.header = "#00ff5f bold"
| MCP:vFs1pM | output.odd-row = ""
| MCP:vFs1pM | output.even-row = ""
| MCP:vFs1pM | output.null = "#808080"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Named queries are queries you can execute by name.
| MCP:vFs1pM | [named queries]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # ver = "SELECT version()"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Here's where you can provide a list of connection string aliases.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # You can use it by passing the -D option. `pgcli -D example_dsn`
| MCP:vFs1pM | [alias_dsn]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # example_dsn = postgresql://[user[:password]@][netloc][:port][/dbname]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Initial commands to execute when connecting to any database.
| MCP:vFs1pM | [init-commands]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # example = "SET search_path TO myschema"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Initial commands to execute when connecting to a DSN alias.
| MCP:vFs1pM | [alias_dsn.init-commands]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # example_dsn = "SET search_path TO otherschema; SET timezone TO 'UTC'"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Format for number representation
| MCP:vFs1pM | # for decimal "d" - 12345678, ",d" - 12,345,678
| MCP:vFs1pM | # for float "g" - 123456.78, ",g" - 123,456.78
| MCP:vFs1pM | [data_formats]
| MCP:vFs1pM | decimal = ""
| MCP:vFs1pM | float = ""
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Per column formats for date/timestamp columns
| MCP:vFs1pM | [column_date_formats]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # use strftime format, e.g.
| MCP:vFs1pM | # created = "%Y-%m-%d"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Per host ssh tunnel configuration
| MCP:vFs1pM | [ssh tunnels]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # ^example.*\.host$ = myuser:mypasswd@my.tunnel.com:4000
| MCP:vFs1pM | # .*\.net = another.tunnel.com
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | # Per dsn_alias ssh tunnel configuration
| MCP:vFs1pM | [dsn ssh tunnels]
| MCP:vFs1pM | # ^example_dsn$  = myuser:mypasswd@my.tunnel.com:4000

| MCP:vFs1pM | import shutil
| MCP:vFs1pM | import os
| MCP:vFs1pM | import platform
| MCP:vFs1pM | from os.path import expanduser, exists, dirname
| MCP:vFs1pM | import re
| MCP:vFs1pM | from typing import TextIO
| MCP:vFs1pM | from configobj import ConfigObj
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def config_location():
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if "XDG_CONFIG_HOME" in os.environ:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return "%s/pgcli/" % expanduser(os.environ["XDG_CONFIG_HOME"])
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif platform.system() == "Windows":
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return os.getenv("USERPROFILE") + "\\AppData\\Local\\dbcli\\pgcli\\"
| MCP:vFs1pM |     else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return expanduser("~/.config/pgcli/")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def load_config(usr_cfg, def_cfg=None):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # avoid config merges when possible. For writing, we need an umerged config instance.
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # see https://github.com/dbcli/pgcli/issues/1240 and https://github.com/DiffSK/configobj/issues/171
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if def_cfg:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         cfg = ConfigObj()
| MCP:vFs1pM |         cfg.merge(ConfigObj(def_cfg, interpolation=False))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         cfg.merge(ConfigObj(expanduser(usr_cfg), interpolation=False, encoding="utf-8"))
| MCP:vFs1pM |     else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         cfg = ConfigObj(expanduser(usr_cfg), interpolation=False, encoding="utf-8")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     cfg.filename = expanduser(usr_cfg)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return cfg
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def ensure_dir_exists(path):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     parent_dir = expanduser(dirname(path))
| MCP:vFs1pM |     os.makedirs(parent_dir, exist_ok=True)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def write_default_config(source, destination, overwrite=False):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     destination = expanduser(destination)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not overwrite and exists(destination):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     ensure_dir_exists(destination)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     shutil.copyfile(source, destination)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def upgrade_config(config, def_config):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     cfg = load_config(config, def_config)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     cfg.write()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def get_config_filename(pgclirc_file=None):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return pgclirc_file or "%sconfig" % config_location()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def get_config(pgclirc_file=None):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     from pgcli import __file__ as package_root
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     package_root = os.path.dirname(package_root)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     pgclirc_file = get_config_filename(pgclirc_file)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     default_config = os.path.join(package_root, "pgclirc")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     write_default_config(default_config, pgclirc_file)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return load_config(pgclirc_file, default_config)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def get_casing_file(config):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     casing_file = config["main"]["casing_file"]
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if casing_file == "default":
| MCP:vFs1pM |         casing_file = config_location() + "casing"
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return casing_file
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def skip_initial_comment(f_stream: TextIO) -> int:
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """
| MCP:vFs1pM |     Initial comment in ~/.pg_service.conf is not always marked with '#'
| MCP:vFs1pM |     which crashes the parser. This function takes a file object and
| MCP:vFs1pM |     "rewinds" it to the beginning of the first section,
| MCP:vFs1pM |     from where on it can be parsed safely
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     :return: number of skipped lines
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """
| MCP:vFs1pM |     section_regex = r"\s*\["
| MCP:vFs1pM |     pos = f_stream.tell()
| MCP:vFs1pM |     lines_skipped = 0
| MCP:vFs1pM |     while True:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         line = f_stream.readline()
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if line == "":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             break
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if re.match(section_regex, line) is not None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             f_stream.seek(pos)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             break
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             pos += len(line)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             lines_skipped += 1

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def set_default_pager(self, config):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         configured_pager = config["main"].get("pager")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         os_environ_pager = os.environ.get("PAGER")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if configured_pager:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.logger.info('Default pager found in config file: "%s"', configured_pager)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             os.environ["PAGER"] = configured_pager
| MCP:vFs1pM |         elif os_environ_pager:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.logger.info(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 'Default pager found in PAGER environment variable: "%s"',
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 os_environ_pager,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             os.environ["PAGER"] = os_environ_pager
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.logger.info("No default pager found in environment. Using os default pager")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Set default set of less recommended options, if they are not already set.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # They are ignored if pager is different than less.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not os.environ.get("LESS"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             os.environ["LESS"] = "-SRXF"

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _is_named_query_execution(self, text):

| MCP:vFs1pM |             "Toggle verbose errors.",
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def toggle_verbose_errors(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         flag = pattern.strip()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if flag == "on":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.verbose_errors = True
| MCP:vFs1pM |         elif flag == "off":

| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def echo(self, pattern, **_):

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def change_table_format(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:

| MCP:vFs1pM |             msg = f"Table format {pattern} not recognized. Allowed formats:"

| MCP:vFs1pM |                 msg += f"\n\t{table_type}"

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def info_connection(self, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.pgexecute.host.startswith("/"):

| MCP:vFs1pM |             '"%s" on %s at port "%s".' % (self.pgexecute.dbname, self.pgexecute.user, host, self.pgexecute.port),
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def change_db(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pattern:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Get all the parameters in pattern, handling double quotes if any.
| MCP:vFs1pM |             infos = re.findall(r'"[^"]*"|[^"\'\s]+', pattern)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Now removing quotes.
| MCP:vFs1pM |             [s.strip('"') for s in infos]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             infos.extend([None] * (4 - len(infos)))
| MCP:vFs1pM |             db, user, host, port = infos
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.pgexecute.connect(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     database=db,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     user=user,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     host=host,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     port=port,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     **self.pgexecute.extra_args,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except OperationalError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.echo("Previous connection kept")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.pgexecute.connect()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         yield (
| MCP:vFs1pM |             None,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             None,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             None,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             'You are now connected to database "%s" as user "%s"' % (self.pgexecute.dbname, self.pgexecute.user),
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def execute_from_file(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not pattern:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             message = "\\i: missing required argument"
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return [(None, None, None, message, "", False, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             with open(os.path.expanduser(pattern), encoding="utf-8") as f:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 query = f.read()
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except OSError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return [(None, None, None, str(e), "", False, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.destructive_warning:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if (
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.destructive_statements_require_transaction
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and not self.pgexecute.valid_transaction()
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and is_destructive(query, self.destructive_warning)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             ):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 message = "Destructive statements must be run within a transaction. Command execution stopped."
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return [(None, None, None, message)]
| MCP:vFs1pM |             destroy = confirm_destructive_query(query, self.destructive_warning, self.dsn_alias)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if destroy is False:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 message = "Wise choice. Command execution stopped."
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return [(None, None, None, message)]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         on_error_resume = self.on_error == "RESUME"
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return self.pgexecute.run(
| MCP:vFs1pM |             query,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.pgspecial,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             on_error_resume=on_error_resume,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             explain_mode=self.explain_mode,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def write_to_logfile(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not pattern:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.log_file = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |             message = "Logfile capture disabled"
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return [(None, None, None, message, "", True, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         log_file = pathlib.Path(pattern).expanduser().absolute()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             with open(log_file, "a+"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 pass  # ensure writeable
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except OSError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.log_file = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |             message = str(e) + "\nLogfile capture disabled"
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return [(None, None, None, message, "", False, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.log_file = str(log_file)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         message = 'Writing to file "%s"' % self.log_file
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return [(None, None, None, message, "", True, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def write_to_file(self, pattern, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not pattern:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.output_file = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |             message = "File output disabled"
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return [(None, None, None, message, "", True, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         filename = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(pattern))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not os.path.isfile(filename):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 open(filename, "w").close()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except OSError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.output_file = None

| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return [(None, None, None, message, "", False, True)]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.output_file = filename
| MCP:vFs1pM |         message = 'Writing to file "%s"' % self.output_file
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return [(None, None, None, message, "", True, True)]

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def initialize_logging(self):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         log_file = self.config["main"]["log_file"]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if log_file == "default":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             log_file = config_location() + "log"
| MCP:vFs1pM |         ensure_dir_exists(log_file)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         log_level = self.config["main"]["log_level"]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Disable logging if value is NONE by switching to a no-op handler.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Set log level to a high value so it doesn't even waste cycles getting called.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if log_level.upper() == "NONE":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             handler = logging.NullHandler()
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             handler = logging.FileHandler(os.path.expanduser(log_file))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         level_map = {
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "CRITICAL": logging.CRITICAL,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "ERROR": logging.ERROR,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "WARNING": logging.WARNING,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "INFO": logging.INFO,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "DEBUG": logging.DEBUG,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "NONE": logging.CRITICAL,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         }
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         log_level = level_map[log_level.upper()]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         formatter = logging.Formatter("%(asctime)s (%(process)d/%(threadName)s) %(name)s %(levelname)s - %(message)s")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         handler.setFormatter(formatter)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         root_logger = logging.getLogger("pgcli")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         root_logger.addHandler(handler)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         root_logger.setLevel(log_level)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         root_logger.debug("Initializing pgcli logging.")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         root_logger.debug("Log file %r.", log_file)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgspecial_logger = logging.getLogger("pgspecial")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgspecial_logger.addHandler(handler)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgspecial_logger.setLevel(log_level)
| MCP:vFs1pM |

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def connect_uri(self, uri):

| MCP:vFs1pM |     def connect(self, database="", host="", user="", port="", passwd="", dsn="", **kwargs):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Connect to the database.
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not user:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             user = getuser()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not database:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             database = user
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         kwargs.setdefault("application_name", self.application_name)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # If password prompt is not forced but no password is provided, try
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # getting it from environment variable.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not self.force_passwd_prompt and not passwd:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Check if DSN contains a password - if so, don't use PGPASSWORD
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 parsed_dsn = conninfo_to_dict(dsn)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if "password" not in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     passwd = os.environ.get("PGPASSWORD", "")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 passwd = os.environ.get("PGPASSWORD", "")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Prompt for a password immediately if requested via the -W flag. This
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # avoids wasting time trying to connect to the database and catching a
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # no-password exception.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # If we successfully parsed a password from a URI, there's no need to
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # prompt for it, even with the -W flag
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.force_passwd_prompt and not passwd:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             passwd = click.prompt("Password for %s" % user, hide_input=True, show_default=False, type=str)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         key = f"{user}@{host}"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not passwd and auth.keyring:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             passwd = auth.keyring_get_password(key)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         def should_ask_for_password(exc):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Prompt for a password after 1st attempt to connect
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # fails. Don't prompt if the -w flag is supplied
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.never_passwd_prompt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return False
| MCP:vFs1pM |             error_msg = exc.args[0]
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if "no password supplied" in error_msg:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return True
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if "password authentication failed" in error_msg:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return True
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             parsed_dsn = conninfo_to_dict(dsn)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if "host" in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 host = parsed_dsn["host"]
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if "port" in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 port = parsed_dsn["port"]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.dsn_alias and self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config and not self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             for dsn_regex, tunnel_url in self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config.items():
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if re.search(dsn_regex, self.dsn_alias):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.ssh_tunnel_url = tunnel_url
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     break
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.ssh_tunnel_config and not self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             for db_host_regex, tunnel_url in self.ssh_tunnel_config.items():
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if re.search(db_host_regex, host):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.ssh_tunnel_url = tunnel_url
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     break
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # We add the protocol as urlparse doesn't find it by itself
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if "://" not in self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.ssh_tunnel_url = f"ssh://{self.ssh_tunnel_url}"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             tunnel_info = urlparse(self.ssh_tunnel_url)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             params = {
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "local_bind_address": ("127.0.0.1",),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "remote_bind_address": (host, int(port or 5432)),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "ssh_address_or_host": (tunnel_info.hostname, tunnel_info.port or 22),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "logger": self.logger,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             }
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if tunnel_info.username:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 params["ssh_username"] = tunnel_info.username
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if tunnel_info.password:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 params["ssh_password"] = tunnel_info.password
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Hack: sshtunnel adds a console handler to the logger, so we revert handlers.
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # We can remove this when https://github.com/pahaz/sshtunnel/pull/250 is merged.
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger_handlers = self.logger.handlers.copy()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.ssh_tunnel = sshtunnel.SSHTunnelForwarder(**params)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.ssh_tunnel.start()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except Exception as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.logger.handlers = logger_handlers
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.logger.error("traceback: %r", traceback.format_exc())
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 sys.exit(1)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.logger.handlers = logger_handlers
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             atexit.register(self.ssh_tunnel.stop)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             host = "127.0.0.1"
| MCP:vFs1pM |             port = self.ssh_tunnel.local_bind_ports[0]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 dsn = make_conninfo(dsn, host=host, port=port)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Attempt to connect to the database.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Note that passwd may be empty on the first attempt. If connection
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # fails because of a missing or incorrect password, but we're allowed to
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # prompt for a password (no -w flag), prompt for a passwd and try again.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 pgexecute = PGExecute(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     database,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     user,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     passwd,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     host,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     port,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     dsn,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     notify_callback,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     **kwargs,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except (OperationalError, InterfaceError) as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if should_ask_for_password(e):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     passwd = click.prompt(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         "Password for %s" % user,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         hide_input=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         show_default=False,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         type=str,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     )
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     pgexecute = PGExecute(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         database,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         user,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         passwd,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         host,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         port,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         dsn,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         notify_callback,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         **kwargs,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     )
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     raise e
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if passwd and auth.keyring:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 auth.keyring_set_password(key, passwd)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except Exception as e:  # Connecting to a database could fail.
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.logger.debug("Database connection failed: %r.", e)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.logger.error("traceback: %r", traceback.format_exc())
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             sys.exit(1)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.pgexecute = pgexecute
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def handle_editor_command(self, text):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         r"""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         Editor command is any query that is prefixed or suffixed
| MCP:vFs1pM |         by a '\e'. The reason for a while loop is because a user
| MCP:vFs1pM |         might edit a query multiple times.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         For eg:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "select * from \e"<enter> to edit it in vim, then come
| MCP:vFs1pM |         back to the prompt with the edited query "select * from
| MCP:vFs1pM |         blah where q = 'abc'\e" to edit it again.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         :param text: Document
| MCP:vFs1pM |         :return: Document
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """
| MCP:vFs1pM |         editor_command = special.editor_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         while editor_command:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if editor_command == "\\e":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 filename = special.get_filename(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 query = special.get_editor_query(text) or self.get_last_query()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:  # \ev or \ef
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 filename = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 spec = text.split()[1]
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if editor_command == "\\ev":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     query = self.pgexecute.view_definition(spec)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 elif editor_command == "\\ef":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     query = self.pgexecute.function_definition(spec)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             sql, message = special.open_external_editor(filename, sql=query)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if message:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Something went wrong. Raise an exception and bail.
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 raise RuntimeError(message)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             while True:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     text = self.prompt_app.prompt(default=sql)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     break
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     sql = ""
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             editor_command = special.editor_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return text
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def execute_command(self, text, handle_closed_connection=True):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         logger = self.logger
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         query = MetaQuery(query=text, successful=False)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.destructive_warning:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if (
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.destructive_statements_require_transaction
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     and not self.pgexecute.valid_transaction()
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     and is_destructive(text, self.destructive_warning)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 ):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     click.secho("Destructive statements must be run within a transaction.")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     raise KeyboardInterrupt
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 destroy = confirm_destructive_query(text, self.destructive_warning, self.dsn_alias)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if destroy is False:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     click.secho("Wise choice!")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     raise KeyboardInterrupt
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 elif destroy:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     click.secho("Your call!")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             output, query = self._evaluate_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.destructive_warning_restarts_connection:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Restart connection to the database
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.pgexecute.connect()
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 logger.debug("cancelled query and restarted connection, sql: %r", text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho("cancelled query and restarted connection", err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 logger.debug("cancelled query, sql: %r", text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho("cancelled query", err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except NotImplementedError:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho("Not Yet Implemented.", fg="yellow")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except OperationalError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.error("sql: %r, error: %r", text, e)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.error("traceback: %r", traceback.format_exc())
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if handle_closed_connection:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self._handle_server_closed_connection(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except (PgCliQuitError, EOFError):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             raise
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except Exception as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.error("sql: %r, error: %r", text, e)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.error("traceback: %r", traceback.format_exc())
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if self.output_file and not text.startswith(("\\o ", "\\log-file", "\\? ", "\\echo ")):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         with open(self.output_file, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             should_hide = (
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 self.hide_named_query_text
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 and query.is_special
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 and query.successful
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 and self._is_named_query_execution(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             if not should_hide:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 click.echo(text, file=f)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             click.echo("\n".join(output), file=f)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             click.echo("", file=f)  # extra newline
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     except OSError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     if output:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         self.echo_via_pager("\n".join(output))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Log to file in addition to normal output
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if self.log_file and not text.startswith(("\\o ", "\\log-file", "\\? ", "\\echo ")) and not text.strip() == "":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         with open(self.log_file, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             click.echo(dt.datetime.now().isoformat(), file=f)  # timestamp log
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             should_hide = (
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 self.hide_named_query_text
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 and query.is_special
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 and query.successful
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 and self._is_named_query_execution(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             if not should_hide:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                                 click.echo(text, file=f)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             click.echo("\n".join(output), file=f)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             click.echo("", file=f)  # extra newline
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     except OSError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 pass
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.pgspecial.timing_enabled:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Only add humanized time display if > 1 second
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if query.total_time > 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     print(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         "Time: %0.03fs (%s), executed in: %0.03fs (%s)"
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         % (
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             query.total_time,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             duration_in_words(query.total_time),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             query.execution_time,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                             duration_in_words(query.execution_time),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         )
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     )
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     print("Time: %0.03fs" % query.total_time)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Check if we need to update completions, in order of most
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # to least drastic changes
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if query.db_changed:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.completer.reset_completions()
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.refresh_completions(persist_priorities="keywords")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             elif query.meta_changed:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.refresh_completions(persist_priorities="all")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             elif query.path_changed:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 logger.debug("Refreshing search path")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.completer.set_search_path(self.pgexecute.search_path())
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 logger.debug("Search path: %r", self.completer.search_path)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return query
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _check_ongoing_transaction_and_allow_quitting(self):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Return whether we can really quit, possibly by asking the
| MCP:vFs1pM |         user to confirm so if there is an ongoing transaction.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not self.pgexecute.valid_transaction():
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return True
| MCP:vFs1pM |         while 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 choice = click.prompt(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     "A transaction is ongoing. Choose `c` to COMMIT, `r` to ROLLBACK, `a` to abort exit, `force` to exit anyway.",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     default="a",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except click.Abort:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Print newline if user aborts with `^C`, otherwise
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # pgcli's prompt will be printed on the same line
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # (just after the confirmation prompt).
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.echo(None, err=False)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 choice = "a"
| MCP:vFs1pM |             choice = choice.lower()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if choice == "a":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return False  # do not quit
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if choice == "force":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return True  # quit anyway
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if choice == "c":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 query = self.execute_command("commit")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return query.successful  # quit only if query is successful
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if choice == "r":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 query = self.execute_command("rollback")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 return query.successful  # quit only if query is successful
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def run_cli(self):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         logger = self.logger
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         history_file = self.config["main"]["history_file"]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if history_file == "default":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             history_file = config_location() + "history"
| MCP:vFs1pM |         history = FileHistory(os.path.expanduser(history_file))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.refresh_completions(history=history, persist_priorities="none")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.prompt_app = self._build_cli(history)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not self.less_chatty:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             print("Server: PostgreSQL", self.pgexecute.server_version)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             print("Version:", __version__)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             print("Home: https://pgcli.com")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             while True:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     text = self.prompt_app.prompt()
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     continue
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 except EOFError:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     if not self._check_ongoing_transaction_and_allow_quitting():
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         continue
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     raise
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     text = self.handle_editor_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 except RuntimeError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     logger.error("sql: %r, error: %r", text, e)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     logger.error("traceback: %r", traceback.format_exc())
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     continue
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.handle_watch_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 except PgCliQuitError:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     if not self._check_ongoing_transaction_and_allow_quitting():
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         continue
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     raise
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.now = dt.datetime.today()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Allow PGCompleter to learn user's preferred keywords, etc.
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.completer.extend_query_history(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except (PgCliQuitError, EOFError):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if not self.less_chatty:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 print("Goodbye!")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def handle_watch_command(self, text):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Initialize default metaquery in case execution fails
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.watch_command, timing = special.get_watch_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # If we run \watch without a command, apply it to the last query run.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.watch_command is not None and not self.watch_command.strip():
| MCP:vFs1pM |             try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.watch_command = self.query_history[-1].query
| MCP:vFs1pM |             except IndexError:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho("\\watch cannot be used with an empty query", err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.watch_command = None
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # If there's a command to \watch, run it in a loop.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.watch_command:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             while self.watch_command:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     query = self.execute_command(self.watch_command)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     click.echo(f"Waiting for {timing} seconds before repeating")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     sleep(timing)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     self.watch_command = None
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Otherwise, execute it as a regular command.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             query = self.execute_command(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.query_history.append(query)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _build_cli(self, history):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         key_bindings = pgcli_bindings(self)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         def get_message():
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.dsn_alias and self.prompt_dsn_format is not None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 prompt_format = self.prompt_dsn_format
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 prompt_format = self.prompt_format
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             prompt = self.get_prompt(prompt_format)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if prompt_format == self.default_prompt and len(prompt) > self.max_len_prompt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 prompt = self.get_prompt("\\d> ")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             prompt = prompt.replace("\\x1b", "\x1b")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return ANSI(prompt)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         def get_continuation(width, line_number, is_soft_wrap):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             continuation = self.multiline_continuation_char * (width - 1) + " "
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return [("class:continuation", continuation)]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         get_toolbar_tokens = create_toolbar_tokens_func(self)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.wider_completion_menu:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             complete_style = CompleteStyle.MULTI_COLUMN
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             complete_style = CompleteStyle.COLUMN
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             prompt_app = PromptSession(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 lexer=PygmentsLexer(PostgresLexer),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 reserve_space_for_menu=self.min_num_menu_lines,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 message=get_message,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 prompt_continuation=get_continuation,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 bottom_toolbar=get_toolbar_tokens if self.show_bottom_toolbar else None,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 complete_style=complete_style,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 input_processors=[
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     # Highlight matching brackets while editing.
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     ConditionalProcessor(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         processor=HighlightMatchingBracketProcessor(chars="[](){}"),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         filter=HasFocus(DEFAULT_BUFFER) & ~IsDone(),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     ),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     # Render \t as 4 spaces instead of "^I"
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     TabsProcessor(char1=" ", char2=" "),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 ],
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 auto_suggest=AutoSuggestFromHistory() if self.auto_suggest else None,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 tempfile_suffix=".sql",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # N.b. pgcli's multi-line mode controls submit-on-Enter (which
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # overrides the default behaviour of prompt_toolkit) and is
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # distinct from prompt_toolkit's multiline mode here, which
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # controls layout/display of the prompt/buffer
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 multiline=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 history=history,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 completer=ThreadedCompleter(DynamicCompleter(lambda: self.completer)),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 complete_while_typing=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 style=style_factory(self.syntax_style, self.cli_style),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 include_default_pygments_style=False,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 key_bindings=key_bindings,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 enable_open_in_editor=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 enable_system_prompt=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 enable_suspend=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 editing_mode=EditingMode.VI if self.vi_mode else EditingMode.EMACS,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 search_ignore_case=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 cursor=ModalCursorShapeConfig(),
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return prompt_app
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _should_limit_output(self, sql, cur):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """returns True if the output should be truncated, False otherwise."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.explain_mode:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return False
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not is_select(sql):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return not self._has_limit(sql) and self.row_limit != 0 and cur and cur.rowcount > self.row_limit
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _has_limit(self, sql):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not sql:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return False
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return "limit " in sql.lower()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _limit_output(self, cur):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         limit = min(self.row_limit, cur.rowcount)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         new_cur = itertools.islice(cur, limit)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         new_status = "SELECT " + str(limit)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         click.secho("The result was limited to %s rows" % limit, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return new_cur, new_status
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _evaluate_command(self, text):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Used to run a command entered by the user during CLI operation
| MCP:vFs1pM |         (Puts the E in REPL)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         returns (results, MetaQuery)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """
| MCP:vFs1pM |         logger = self.logger
| MCP:vFs1pM |         logger.debug("sql: %r", text)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # set query to formatter in order to parse table name
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self.formatter.query = text
| MCP:vFs1pM |         all_success = True
| MCP:vFs1pM |         meta_changed = False  # CREATE, ALTER, DROP, etc
| MCP:vFs1pM |         mutated = False  # INSERT, DELETE, etc
| MCP:vFs1pM |         db_changed = False
| MCP:vFs1pM |         path_changed = False
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |         total = 0
| MCP:vFs1pM |         execution = 0
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Run the query.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         start = time()
| MCP:vFs1pM |         on_error_resume = self.on_error == "RESUME"
| MCP:vFs1pM |         res = self.pgexecute.run(
| MCP:vFs1pM |             text,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.pgspecial,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             lambda x: exception_formatter(x, self.verbose_errors),
| MCP:vFs1pM |             on_error_resume,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             explain_mode=self.explain_mode,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         is_special = None
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         for title, cur, headers, status, sql, success, is_special in res:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.debug("headers: %r", headers)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.debug("rows: %r", cur)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.debug("status: %r", status)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self._should_limit_output(sql, cur):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 cur, status = self._limit_output(cur)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.pgspecial.auto_expand or self.auto_expand:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 max_width = self.prompt_app.output.get_size().columns
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 max_width = None
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             expanded = self.pgspecial.expanded_output or self.expanded_output
| MCP:vFs1pM |             settings = OutputSettings(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 table_format=self.table_format,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 dcmlfmt=self.decimal_format,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 floatfmt=self.float_format,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 column_date_formats=self.column_date_formats,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 missingval=self.null_string,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 expanded=expanded,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 max_width=max_width,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 case_function=(self.completer.case if self.settings["case_column_headers"] else lambda x: x),
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 style_output=self.style_output,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 max_field_width=self.max_field_width,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Hide query text for named queries in quiet mode
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if (
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.hide_named_query_text
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and is_special
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and success
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and self._is_named_query_execution(text)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and title
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 and title.startswith("> ")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             ):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 title = None
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             execution = time() - start
| MCP:vFs1pM |             formatted = format_output(title, cur, headers, status, settings, self.explain_mode)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             output.extend(formatted)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             total = time() - start
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # Keep track of whether any of the queries are mutating or changing
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # the database
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if success:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 mutated = mutated or is_mutating(status)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 db_changed = db_changed or has_change_db_cmd(sql)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 meta_changed = meta_changed or has_meta_cmd(sql)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 path_changed = path_changed or has_change_path_cmd(sql)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 all_success = False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         meta_query = MetaQuery(
| MCP:vFs1pM |             text,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             all_success,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             total,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             execution,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             meta_changed,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             db_changed,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             path_changed,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             mutated,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             is_special,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return output, meta_query
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _handle_server_closed_connection(self, text):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Used during CLI execution."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho("Reconnecting...", fg="green")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.pgexecute.connect()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho("Reconnected!", fg="green")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except OperationalError as e:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho("Reconnect Failed", fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(str(e), err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             retry = self.auto_retry_closed_connection or confirm("Run the query from before reconnecting?")
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if retry:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho("Running query...", fg="green")
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Don't get stuck in a retry loop
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 self.execute_command(text, handle_closed_connection=False)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def refresh_completions(self, history=None, persist_priorities="all"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Refresh outdated completions
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         :param history: A prompt_toolkit.history.FileHistory object. Used to
| MCP:vFs1pM |                         load keyword and identifier preferences
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         :param persist_priorities: 'all' or 'keywords'
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         callback = functools.partial(self._on_completions_refreshed, persist_priorities=persist_priorities)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return self.completion_refresher.refresh(
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.pgexecute,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.pgspecial,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             callback,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             history=history,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             settings=self.settings,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _on_completions_refreshed(self, new_completer, persist_priorities):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         self._swap_completer_objects(new_completer, persist_priorities)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.prompt_app:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # After refreshing, redraw the CLI to clear the statusbar
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # "Refreshing completions..." indicator
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.prompt_app.app.invalidate()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def _swap_completer_objects(self, new_completer, persist_priorities):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Swap the completer object with the newly created completer.
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         persist_priorities is a string specifying how the old completer's
| MCP:vFs1pM |         learned prioritizer should be transferred to the new completer.
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |           'none'     - The new prioritizer is left in a new/clean state
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |           'all'      - The new prioritizer is updated to exactly reflect
| MCP:vFs1pM |                        the old one
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |           'keywords' - The new prioritizer is updated with old keyword
| MCP:vFs1pM |                        priorities, but not any other.
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """
| MCP:vFs1pM |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             old_completer = self.completer
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.completer = new_completer
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if persist_priorities == "all":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Just swap over the entire prioritizer
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 new_completer.prioritizer = old_completer.prioritizer
| MCP:vFs1pM |             elif persist_priorities == "keywords":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Swap over the entire prioritizer, but clear name priorities,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # leaving learned keyword priorities alone
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 new_completer.prioritizer = old_completer.prioritizer
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 new_completer.prioritizer.clear_names()
| MCP:vFs1pM |             elif persist_priorities == "none":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # Leave the new prioritizer as is
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 pass
| MCP:vFs1pM |             self.completer = new_completer
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def get_completions(self, text, cursor_positition):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return self.completer.get_completions(Document(text=text, cursor_position=cursor_positition), None)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def get_prompt(self, string):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # should be before replacing \\d
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\dsn_alias", self.dsn_alias or "")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\t", self.now.strftime("%x %X"))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\u", self.pgexecute.user or "(none)")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\H", self.pgexecute.host or "(none)")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\h", self.pgexecute.short_host or "(none)")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\d", self.pgexecute.dbname or "(none)")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace(
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "\\p",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             str(self.pgexecute.port) if self.pgexecute.port is not None else "5432",
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\i", str(self.pgexecute.pid) or "(none)")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\#", "#" if self.pgexecute.superuser else ">")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\n", "\n")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         string = string.replace("\\T", self.pgexecute.transaction_indicator)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return string
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def get_last_query(self):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Get the last query executed or None."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return self.query_history[-1][0] if self.query_history else None
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def is_too_wide(self, line):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Will this line be too wide to fit into terminal?"""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not self.prompt_app:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return False
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return len(COLOR_CODE_REGEX.sub("", line)) > self.prompt_app.output.get_size().columns
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def is_too_tall(self, lines):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         """Are there too many lines to fit into terminal?"""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not self.prompt_app:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return False
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return len(lines) >= (self.prompt_app.output.get_size().rows - 4)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def echo_via_pager(self, text, color=None):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if self.pgspecial.pager_config == PAGER_OFF or self.watch_command:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.echo(text, color=color)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         elif self.pgspecial.pager_config == PAGER_LONG_OUTPUT and self.table_format != "csv":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             lines = text.split("\n")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # The last 4 lines are reserved for the pgcli menu and padding
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if self.is_too_tall(lines) or any(self.is_too_wide(l) for l in lines):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.echo_via_pager(text, color=color)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.echo(text, color=color)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.echo_via_pager(text, color)
| MCP:vFs1pM |

| MCP:vFs1pM |     config_dir = os.path.dirname(config_location())
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not os.path.exists(config_dir):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         os.makedirs(config_dir)

| MCP:vFs1pM |     # Migrate the config file from old location.
| MCP:vFs1pM |     config_full_path = config_location() + "config"
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if os.path.exists(os.path.expanduser("~/.pgclirc")):

| MCP:vFs1pM |             shutil.move(os.path.expanduser("~/.pgclirc"), config_full_path)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             print("Config file (~/.pgclirc) moved to new location", config_full_path)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:

| MCP:vFs1pM |                 config_full_path,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if list_dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             cfg = load_config(pgclirc, config_full_path)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             for alias in cfg["alias_dsn"]:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 click.secho(alias + " : " + cfg["alias_dsn"][alias])
| MCP:vFs1pM |             sys.exit(0)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except Exception:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "Invalid DSNs found in the config file. Please check the \"[alias_dsn]\" section in pgclirc.",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 fg="red",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             sys.exit(1)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if ssh_tunnel and not SSH_TUNNEL_SUPPORT:

| MCP:vFs1pM |             'Cannot open SSH tunnel, "sshtunnel" package was not found. '
| MCP:vFs1pM |             "Please install pgcli with `pip install pgcli[sshtunnel]` if you want SSH tunnel support.",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             fg="red",
| MCP:vFs1pM |         )
| MCP:vFs1pM |         sys.exit(1)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     pgcli = PGCli(
| MCP:vFs1pM |         prompt_passwd,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         never_prompt,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgclirc_file=pgclirc,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         row_limit=row_limit,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         application_name=application_name,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         single_connection=single_connection,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         less_chatty=less_chatty,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         prompt=prompt,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         prompt_dsn=prompt_dsn,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         auto_vertical_output=auto_vertical_output,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         warn=warn,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         ssh_tunnel_url=ssh_tunnel,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         log_file=log_file,
| MCP:vFs1pM |     )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # Choose which ever one has a valid value.
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if dbname_opt and dbname:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # work as psql: when database is given as option and argument use the argument as user
| MCP:vFs1pM |         username = dbname
| MCP:vFs1pM |     database = dbname_opt or dbname or ""
| MCP:vFs1pM |     user = username_opt or username
| MCP:vFs1pM |     service = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if database.startswith("service="):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         service = database[8:]
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif os.getenv("PGSERVICE") is not None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         service = os.getenv("PGSERVICE")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # because option --ping, --list or -l are not supposed to have a db name
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if list_databases or ping_database:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         database = "postgres"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     cfg = load_config(pgclirc, config_full_path)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if dsn != "":
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             dsn_config = cfg["alias_dsn"][dsn]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except KeyError:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(

| MCP:vFs1pM |                 fg="red",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             sys.exit(1)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except Exception:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "Invalid DSNs found in the config file. Please check the \"[alias_dsn]\" section in pgclirc.",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 fg="red",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             sys.exit(1)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgcli.dsn_alias = dsn
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgcli.connect_uri(dsn_config)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif "://" in database:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgcli.connect_uri(database)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif "=" in database and service is None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgcli.connect_dsn(database, user=user)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif service is not None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgcli.connect_service(service, user)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         pgcli.connect(database, host, user, port)

| MCP:vFs1pM |     if "use_local_timezone" not in cfg["main"] or cfg["main"].as_bool("use_local_timezone"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         server_tz = pgcli.pgexecute.get_timezone()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         def echo_error(msg: str):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 "Failed to determine the local time zone",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 fg="yellow",

| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 msg,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 fg="yellow",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 f"Continuing with the default time zone as preset by the server ({server_tz})",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 fg="yellow",
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             click.secho(

| MCP:vFs1pM |                 err=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 dim=True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         local_tz = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:

| MCP:vFs1pM |             if local_tz is None:

| MCP:vFs1pM |             elif local_tz != server_tz:

| MCP:vFs1pM |                 pgcli.pgexecute.set_timezone(local_tz)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except ZoneInfoNotFoundError as e:

| MCP:vFs1pM |     # Merge init-commands: global, DSN-specific, then CLI-provided
| MCP:vFs1pM |     init_cmds = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # 1) Global init-commands
| MCP:vFs1pM |     global_section = pgcli.config.get("init-commands", {})
| MCP:vFs1pM |     for _, val in global_section.items():
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             init_cmds.extend(val)

| MCP:vFs1pM |     # 2) DSN-specific init-commands
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if dsn:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         alias_section = pgcli.config.get("alias_dsn.init-commands", {})
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if dsn in alias_section:

| MCP:vFs1pM |     if init_cmds:

| MCP:vFs1pM |     pgcli.logger.debug(

| MCP:vFs1pM |     pgcli.run_cli()

| MCP:vFs1pM | def obfuscate_process_password():
| MCP:vFs1pM |     process_title = setproctitle.getproctitle()
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if "://" in process_title:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         process_title = re.sub(r":(.*):(.*)@", r":\1:xxxx@", process_title)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif "=" in process_title:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         process_title = re.sub(r"password=(.+?)((\s[a-zA-Z]+=)|$)", r"password=xxxx\2", process_title)

| MCP:vFs1pM |     setproctitle.setproctitle(process_title)

| MCP:vFs1pM | def has_meta_cmd(query):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Determines if the completion needs a refresh by checking if the sql
| MCP:vFs1pM |     statement is an alter, create, drop, commit or rollback."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         first_token = query.split()[0]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if first_token.lower() in ("alter", "create", "drop", "commit", "rollback"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return True
| MCP:vFs1pM |     except Exception:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def has_change_db_cmd(query):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Determines if the statement is a database switch such as 'use' or '\\c'"""
| MCP:vFs1pM |     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         first_token = query.split()[0]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if first_token.lower() in ("use", "\\c", "\\connect"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return True
| MCP:vFs1pM |     except Exception:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def has_change_path_cmd(sql):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Determines if the search_path should be refreshed by checking if the
| MCP:vFs1pM |     sql has 'set search_path'."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return "set search_path" in sql.lower()
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def is_mutating(status):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Determines if the statement is mutating based on the status."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not status:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return False
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     mutating = {"insert", "update", "delete"}
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return status.split(None, 1)[0].lower() in mutating
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def is_select(status):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Returns true if the first word in status is 'select'."""
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not status:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return False
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return status.split(None, 1)[0].lower() == "select"
| MCP:vFs1pM |
