| MCP:vFs1pM | def format_output(title, cur, headers, status, settings, explain_mode=False):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     output = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |     expanded = settings.expanded or settings.table_format == "vertical"
| MCP:vFs1pM |     table_format = "vertical" if settings.expanded else settings.table_format
| MCP:vFs1pM |     max_width = settings.max_width
| MCP:vFs1pM |     case_function = settings.case_function
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if explain_mode:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         formatter = ExplainOutputFormatter(max_width or 100)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         formatter = TabularOutputFormatter(format_name=table_format)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def format_array(val):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if val is None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return settings.missingval
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not isinstance(val, list):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             return val
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return "{" + ",".join(str(format_array(e)) for e in val) + "}"
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def format_arrays(data, headers, **_):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         data = list(data)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         for row in data:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             row[:] = [format_array(val) if isinstance(val, list) else val for val in row]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return data, headers
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def format_status(cur, status):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # redshift does not return rowcount as part of status.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # See https://github.com/dbcli/pgcli/issues/1320
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if cur and hasattr(cur, "rowcount") and cur.rowcount is not None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if status and not status.endswith(str(cur.rowcount)):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 status += " %s" % cur.rowcount
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return status
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     output_kwargs = {
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "sep_title": "RECORD {n}",
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "sep_character": "-",
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "sep_length": (1, 25),
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "missing_value": settings.missingval,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "integer_format": settings.dcmlfmt,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "float_format": settings.floatfmt,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "column_date_formats": settings.column_date_formats,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "preprocessors": (format_numbers, format_arrays),
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "disable_numparse": True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "preserve_whitespace": True,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "style": settings.style_output,
| MCP:vFs1pM |         "max_field_width": settings.max_field_width,
| MCP:vFs1pM |     }
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not settings.floatfmt:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output_kwargs["preprocessors"] = (align_decimals,)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if settings.column_date_formats:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output_kwargs["preprocessors"] += (format_timestamps,)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if table_format == "csv":
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # The default CSV dialect is "excel" which is not handling newline values correctly
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # Nevertheless, we want to keep on using "excel" on Windows since it uses '\r\n'
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # as the line terminator
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # https://github.com/dbcli/pgcli/issues/1102
| MCP:vFs1pM |         dialect = "excel" if platform.system() == "Windows" else "unix"
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output_kwargs["dialect"] = dialect
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if title:  # Only print the title if it's not None.
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output.append(title)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if cur:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         headers = [case_function(x) for x in headers]
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if max_width is not None:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             cur = list(cur)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         column_types = None
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if hasattr(cur, "description"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             column_types = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |             for d in cur.description:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 col_type = cur.adapters.types.get(d.type_code)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 type_name = col_type.name if col_type else None
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if type_name in ("numeric", "float4", "float8"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     column_types.append(float)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 if type_name in ("int2", "int4", "int8"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     column_types.append(int)
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                     column_types.append(str)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         formatted = formatter.format_output(cur, headers, **output_kwargs)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if isinstance(formatted, str):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             formatted = iter(formatted.splitlines())
| MCP:vFs1pM |         first_line = next(formatted)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         formatted = itertools.chain([first_line], formatted)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if not explain_mode and not expanded and max_width and len(strip_ansi(first_line)) > max_width and headers:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             formatted = formatter.format_output(
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 cur,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 headers,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 format_name="vertical",
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 column_types=column_types,
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 **output_kwargs,
| MCP:vFs1pM |             )
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if isinstance(formatted, str):
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 formatted = iter(formatted.splitlines())
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output = itertools.chain(output, formatted)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # Only print the status if it's not None
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if status:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         output = itertools.chain(output, [format_status(cur, status)])
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return output
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def parse_service_info(service):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     service = service or os.getenv("PGSERVICE")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     service_file = os.getenv("PGSERVICEFILE")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not service_file:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # try ~/.pg_service.conf (if that exists)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if platform.system() == "Windows":
| MCP:vFs1pM |             service_file = os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR") + "\\pg_service.conf"
| MCP:vFs1pM |         elif os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR"):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             service_file = os.path.join(os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR"), ".pg_service.conf")
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             service_file = os.path.expanduser("~/.pg_service.conf")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not service or not os.path.exists(service_file):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         # nothing to do
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return None, service_file
| MCP:vFs1pM |     with open(service_file, newline="") as f:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         skipped_lines = skip_initial_comment(f)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             service_file_config = ConfigObj(f)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         except ParseError as err:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             err.line_number += skipped_lines
| MCP:vFs1pM |             raise err
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if service not in service_file_config:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return None, service_file
| MCP:vFs1pM |     service_conf = service_file_config.get(service)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return service_conf, service_file
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def duration_in_words(duration_in_seconds: float) -> str:
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if not duration_in_seconds:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return "0 seconds"
| MCP:vFs1pM |     components = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |     hours, remainder = divmod(duration_in_seconds, 3600)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if hours > 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append(f"{int(hours)} hours")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif hours == 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append("1 hour")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     minutes, seconds = divmod(remainder, 60)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if minutes > 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append(f"{int(minutes)} minutes")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif minutes == 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append("1 minute")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     if seconds >= 2:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append(f"{int(seconds)} seconds")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif seconds >= 1:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append("1 second")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     elif seconds:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         components.append(f"{round(seconds, 3)} second")
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return " ".join(components)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | if __name__ == "__main__":
| MCP:vFs1pM |     cli()

| MCP:vFs1pM | def parse_pygments_style(token_name, style_object, style_dict):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Parse token type and style string.
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     :param token_name: str name of Pygments token. Example: "Token.String"
| MCP:vFs1pM |     :param style_object: pygments.style.Style instance to use as base
| MCP:vFs1pM |     :param style_dict: dict of token names and their styles, customized to this cli
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """
| MCP:vFs1pM |     token_type = string_to_tokentype(token_name)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         other_token_type = string_to_tokentype(style_dict[token_name])
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return token_type, style_object.styles[other_token_type]
| MCP:vFs1pM |     except AttributeError:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return token_type, style_dict[token_name]
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM | def style_factory(name, cli_style):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         style = pygments.styles.get_style_by_name(name)
| MCP:vFs1pM |     except ClassNotFound:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         style = pygments.styles.get_style_by_name("native")
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     prompt_styles = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # prompt-toolkit used pygments tokens for styling before, switched to style
| MCP:vFs1pM |     # names in 2.0. Convert old token types to new style names, for backwards compatibility.
| MCP:vFs1pM |     for token in cli_style:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if token.startswith("Token."):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # treat as pygments token (1.0)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             token_type, style_value = parse_pygments_style(token, style, cli_style)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if token_type in TOKEN_TO_PROMPT_STYLE:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 prompt_style = TOKEN_TO_PROMPT_STYLE[token_type]
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 prompt_styles.append((prompt_style, style_value))
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 # we don't want to support tokens anymore
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 logger.error("Unhandled style / class name: %s", token)
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # treat as prompt style name (2.0). See default style names here:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # https://github.com/prompt-toolkit/python-prompt-toolkit/blob/master/src/prompt_toolkit/styles/defaults.py
| MCP:vFs1pM |             prompt_styles.append((token, cli_style[token]))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     override_style = Style([("bottom-toolbar", "noreverse")])
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return merge_styles([style_from_pygments_cls(style), override_style, Style(prompt_styles)])

| MCP:vFs1pM | def style_factory_output(name, cli_style):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     try:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         style = pygments.styles.get_style_by_name(name).styles
| MCP:vFs1pM |     except ClassNotFound:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         style = pygments.styles.get_style_by_name("native").styles
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     for token in cli_style:
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if token.startswith("Token."):
| MCP:vFs1pM |             token_type, style_value = parse_pygments_style(token, style, cli_style)
| MCP:vFs1pM |             style.update({token_type: style_value})
| MCP:vFs1pM |         elif token in PROMPT_STYLE_TO_TOKEN:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             token_type = PROMPT_STYLE_TO_TOKEN[token]
| MCP:vFs1pM |             style.update({token_type: cli_style[token]})
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             # TODO: cli helpers will have to switch to ptk.Style
| MCP:vFs1pM |             logger.error("Unhandled style / class name: %s", token)
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     class OutputStyle(PygmentsStyle):
| MCP:vFs1pM |         default_style = ""
| MCP:vFs1pM |         styles = style
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return OutputStyle

| MCP:vFs1pM | def create_toolbar_tokens_func(pgcli):
| MCP:vFs1pM |     """Return a function that generates the toolbar tokens."""
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     def get_toolbar_tokens():
| MCP:vFs1pM |         result = []
| MCP:vFs1pM |         result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", " "))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.completer.smart_completion:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar.on", "[F2] Smart Completion: ON  "))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar.off", "[F2] Smart Completion: OFF  "))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.multi_line:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar.on", "[F3] Multiline: ON  "))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar.off", "[F3] Multiline: OFF  "))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.multi_line:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             if pgcli.multiline_mode == "safe":
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", " ([Esc] [Enter] to execute]) "))
| MCP:vFs1pM |             else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |                 result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", " (Semi-colon [;] will end the line) "))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.vi_mode:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", "[F4] Vi-mode (" + _get_vi_mode() + ")  "))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", "[F4] Emacs-mode  "))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.explain_mode:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", "[F5] Explain: ON "))
| MCP:vFs1pM |         else:
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", "[F5] Explain: OFF "))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.pgexecute.failed_transaction():
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar.transaction.failed", "     Failed transaction"))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.pgexecute.valid_transaction():
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar.transaction.valid", "     Transaction"))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         if pgcli.completion_refresher.is_refreshing():
| MCP:vFs1pM |             result.append(("class:bottom-toolbar", "     Refreshing completions..."))
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |         return result
| MCP:vFs1pM | 
| MCP:vFs1pM |     return get_toolbar_tokens
